title: veronica pilosa var. surculosa
intro: Veronica pilosa var. surculosa, also known as creeping speedwell, is a low-growing, spreading plant with small blue flowers, belonging to the plantain family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Veronica pilosa var. surculosa include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
